name: Application Modernization
description: Guides teams through assessing and planning the modernization of legacy applications — lift-and-shift, re-platform, re-architect, or rebuild

defaultWeights.label: Migration Risk
defaultWeights.description: Tolerance for breaking changes during modernization — high allows aggressive refactoring

defaultWeights.label: Cloud Cost
defaultWeights.description: Sensitivity to infrastructure costs — high favors pay-as-you-go, low allows reserved capacity

defaultWeights.label: Modern Stack
defaultWeights.description: Preference for cutting-edge frameworks — high adopts latest, low stays on proven LTS

defaultWeights.label: Code Complexity
defaultWeights.description: Tolerance for architectural complexity — high allows microservices, low favors monolith

defaultWeights.label: Delivery Speed
defaultWeights.description: Time-to-market pressure — high ships incremental, low delivers complete rewrite

defaultWeights.label: Team Autonomy
defaultWeights.description: How much teams decide independently — high decentralizes, low standardizes

defaultWeights.label: Data Integrity
defaultWeights.description: Priority on data migration fidelity — high ensures zero data loss, low accepts eventual consistency

defaultWeights.label: Scalability
defaultWeights.description: Design for future growth — high builds for 10x load, low sizes for current needs

nodes.label: Reported Symptoms
nodes.description: What symptoms is the aircraft exhibiting?
nodes.prompt: What primary symptom has been reported?

nodes.label: Flight Phase
nodes.description: When was the symptom first observed?
nodes.prompt: During which flight phase was the symptom observed?

nodes.label: Severity Assessment
nodes.description: How critical is the reported issue?
nodes.prompt: What is the severity level of the fault?

nodes.label: Recent Maintenance
nodes.description: Assumes recent maintenance may be a contributing factor
nodes.assumption: The aircraft had maintenance performed within the last 72 hours. Recent work should be checked for installation errors or incomplete tasks.

nodes.label: System Isolation
nodes.description: Narrow down to the affected aircraft system
nodes.condition: Which aircraft system does the symptom pattern indicate?

nodes.label: Engine Fault Type
nodes.condition: Does the engine exhibit mechanical vibration or performance degradation?

nodes.label: Avionics Fault Scope
nodes.condition: Is the fault isolated to one display/system or affecting multiple avionics buses?

nodes.label: Hydraulic System Check
nodes.condition: Is there visible fluid loss or is this a pressure-only indication?

nodes.label: Engine Mechanical Diagnosis
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an A&P mechanic with IA authorization and 20 years of turbine engine experience. Provide precise, regulation-compliant diagnostic procedures referencing AMM chapters. Safety is paramount — when in doubt, ground the aircraft.

nodes.label: Engine Performance Analysis
nodes.description: FADEC data and trend analysis procedure
nodes.template:
Generate an engine performance degradation analysis plan.
Symptom: {{symptom}}
Flight Phase: {{flightPhase}}
Severity: {{severity}}

Include:
1. FADEC data download and parameter review
2. EGT margin and trend analysis
3. Fuel flow comparison to baseline
4. Compressor wash evaluation
5. Recommended engine trend monitoring schedule
6. Go/No-go decision criteria
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an aircraft engine performance engineer. Provide data-driven analysis procedures with clear numerical thresholds for go/no-go decisions.

nodes.label: Single LRU Replacement
nodes.systemPrompt: You are an avionics technician with authorization on glass cockpit systems. Reference applicable IPC part numbers and FIM procedures.

nodes.label: Avionics Bus Fault Analysis
nodes.template:
Generate a comprehensive avionics bus fault investigation plan.
Symptom: {{symptom}}
Flight Phase: {{flightPhase}}
Severity: {{severity}}

Include:
1. Affected bus identification (ARINC 429 / MIL-STD-1553 / Ethernet)
2. Power supply and circuit breaker audit
3. Data bus analyzer trace procedure
4. Common mode failure analysis
5. Grounding and shielding inspection
6. Recommended aircraft ground time estimate
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a senior avionics systems engineer. Provide systematic bus-level fault isolation procedures. Multi-system faults require grounding until root cause is confirmed.

nodes.label: Hydraulic Leak Investigation
nodes.description: Hydraulic fluid loss diagnosis and repair
nodes.template:
Generate a hydraulic leak investigation and repair procedure.
Symptom: {{symptom}}
Flight Phase: {{flightPhase}}
Severity: {{severity}}

Include:
1. Visual inspection zones (gear bay, flight control actuators, pumps)
2. Fluid quantity trend analysis
3. System pressurization ground test procedure
4. Seal and fitting inspection checklist
5. Fluid type verification and contamination check
6. Return-to-service leak check criteria
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a hydraulics specialist. Hydraulic fluid loss is always a grounding event until the source is identified and repaired. Provide thorough procedures.

nodes.label: Hydraulic Pressure Diagnosis
nodes.description: Pressure anomaly without visible fluid loss
nodes.template:
Generate a hydraulic pressure anomaly diagnosis plan.
Symptom: {{symptom}}
Flight Phase: {{flightPhase}}
Severity: {{severity}}

Include:
1. Pressure transducer verification
2. Pump output test procedure
3. Relief valve and check valve inspection
4. Accumulator pre-charge verification
5. System schematic analysis for internal bypass
6. Minimum dispatch pressure criteria per MEL
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a hydraulic systems engineer. Provide systematic pressure fault isolation procedures with specific test adapter part numbers and pressure ranges.
